title: Reading and Use of English - Part 4
description: For questions 25 – 30, complete the second sentence so that it has a similar meaning to the first sentence, using the word given. Do not change the word given. You must use between three and six words, including the word given. Here is an example (0).

questions.firstSentence: They believe that the criminal is hiding somewhere in the city.
questions.explanation: Tests the personal passive with the continuous infinitive.
questions.secondSentence: The criminal (1) _________ somewhere in the city.

questions.firstSentence: ‘I'm sorry I didn't come to your graduation,’ said my aunt.
questions.explanation: Tests reporting verbs with prepositions and negative gerunds.

questions.firstSentence: I didn't take the map, so I got lost in the forest.
questions.explanation: Tests third conditional for past hypothetical situations.
questions.secondSentence: I wouldn't have got lost if I (1) _________ the map.

questions.firstSentence: I'm sure that Mark wasn't responsible for the error.
questions.explanation: Tests modal perfect for negative deduction in the past.

questions.firstSentence: The concert had only just started when the power failed.
It is not worth buying a new phone if yours still works.
questions.explanation: Tests the gerund expression 'There is no point (in) doing'.
questions.secondSentence: There is (1) _________ a new phone if yours still works.
